evals.prompt: Help me build a lead scoring model for our B2B SaaS product. We're inbound-heavy with a $50K ACV.
evals.expected_output: Full scoring model with 4 dimensions, weighted for inbound motion, calibrated thresholds, and implementation steps

assertions.name: four_dimensions
assertions.description: Builds a model with demographic, firmographic, behavioral, and timing dimensions with specific point values

assertions.name: inbound_weighting
assertions.description: Weights behavioral signals higher (35-50%) appropriate for an inbound-heavy motion

assertions.name: threshold_calibration
assertions.description: Sets MQL and SQL thresholds with guidance on how to calibrate using conversion data

evals.prompt: Our MQL-to-SQL conversion rate is 8%. Our scoring model isn't working. Help me fix it.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis framework, threshold recalibration, and weight adjustment recommendations

assertions.name: diagnosis
assertions.description: Identifies likely causes of low conversion (threshold too low, demographic overweighting, missing decay)

assertions.name: recalibration
assertions.description: Recommends specific threshold and weight adjustments based on conversion data analysis

assertions.name: measurement_plan
assertions.description: Provides specific metrics and a timeline for validating the fixes

evals.prompt: We're a PLG company. How should product usage signals factor into lead scoring?
evals.expected_output: Behavioral-heavy model with product event scoring, PQL threshold, and sales handoff triggers

assertions.name: product_signals
assertions.description: Includes specific product usage events as scoring signals (signups, feature adoption, usage thresholds, team invites)

assertions.name: pql_definition
assertions.description: Defines PQL criteria separately from MQL/SQL with product-specific thresholds

assertions.name: behavioral_heavy
assertions.description: Weights behavioral/product signals at 40-50% of total score, higher than demographic fit
